title: toiyabea alpina
intro: Toiyabea alpina, also known as Sierra mountain tea or mountain tea, is a perennial herb native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. It is a member of the mint family and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, such as colds, fever, and digestive problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving headaches, and improving respiratory function. Toiyabea alpina can be prepared as a tea by steeping the leaves in hot water, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves.
